name: Chocolate Praline Layer Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 package Pillsbury devil's food cake mix
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325 degrees F.
2. In a small heavy saucepan, combine butter, 1/4 cup whipping cream, and brown sugar. Cook over low heat just until the butter is melted, stirring occasionally.
3. Pour the mixture into two (8 or 9 inch) round cake pans and sprinkle evenly with chopped pecans.
4. In a large bowl, combine the cake mix, water, oil, and eggs. Mix on low speed until moistened, then beat at high speed for 2 minutes.
5. Carefully spoon the batter over the pecan mixture.
6. Bake in the preheated oven at 325 degrees F for 35 to 45 minutes, or until the cake springs back when touched lightly in the center. Allow cooling for 5 minutes, then remove from pans and cool completely.
7. In a small bowl, beat 1 3/4 cups whipping cream until soft peaks form. Blend in powdered sugar and vanilla; beat until stiff peaks form.
8. Assemble the cake by placing one layer on a serving plate, praline side up. Spread half of the topping over the first layer.
9. Top with the second layer, praline side up, and spread with the remaining whipping cream.
10. Garnish with whole pecans and/or chocolate curls. Store in the refrigerator. Serves up to 12. For high altitude areas above 3,500 feet, add 2 tablespoons flour to the dry cake mix and increase water to 1 1/3 cups. Increase oven temperature to 350 degrees F and bake for 30 to 35 minutes. Remove immediately from pans.
